title: Angel Muffin

jokes.text: I love theater. Sue me. What am I, crazy?
jokes.setup: Larry defending his love of theater
jokes.punchline: Over-defensive response to an innocent statement
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry neurosis - getting defensive about something completely normal

jokes.text: It's theater, you know? It's all live. It's happening.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he loves theater
jokes.punchline: Stating the most obvious aspects of theater as profound insights
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to over-explain obvious things as if they're revelations

jokes.text: but the girl playing Juliet, my God, she was sensational.
jokes.setup: Larry building up his friend's daughter's performance
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses friend's daughter to praise another girl
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry tactlessness - praising someone else while dismissing his friend's kid

jokes.text: I accidentally erased the tape yesterday. I know, I've been hesitant to tell you.
jokes.setup: Network executives asking to see audition tape
jokes.punchline: Jeff reveals he erased the only evidence of their 'discovery'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's incompetence creating a major problem - typical Curb disaster setup

jokes.text: Oh my God, Jeff, how did you do that? You fuckin' idiot. You're a fuckin' idiot, you know that?
jokes.setup: Jeff admits to erasing the tape
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, brutal verbal assault
jokes.explanation: Larry's lack of filter and immediate harsh judgment of Jeff

jokes.text: Can I tell you something? I am. I am. I'm a fuckin' idiot.
jokes.setup: Larry calling Jeff an idiot
jokes.punchline: Jeff's complete agreement and repetition
jokes.explanation: Jeff's self-deprecating acceptance makes Larry's attack even funnier

jokes.text: Rock concert? Can't stand rock concerts. This is pure torture for me. Honestly, so loud, you know?
jokes.setup: Having to go to Dylan's concert
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic aversion to something normal people enjoy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - complaining about common experiences as personal torture

jokes.text: It's a nightclub. There's not gonna be places to sit. Exactly. And we're gonna have to go backstage and pretend that we liked it.
jokes.setup: Discussing the upcoming concert
jokes.punchline: Predicting they'll have to fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Captures the social obligation to lie about artistic experiences

jokes.text: I got her pregnant. And she got an abortion. And, yeah... One big headache.
jokes.setup: Jeff recommending a dental hygienist
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing a major personal situation as a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Shocking casual revelation delivered with perfect understatement - classic Curb dark humor

jokes.text: She's beautiful, flirted with me. What am I gonna do? What have you got going for you? I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining how the affair started
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal assessment of Jeff's appeal
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to let Jeff have any dignity about his romantic success

jokes.text: Pee before you leave, that's my credo. It's a good credo.
jokes.setup: Larry going to bathroom before leaving
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane advice as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Larry treating bathroom habits as philosophical principles

jokes.text: I was climbing on the rim. Then I was holdin' it up with my foot because that thing can't come down in midstream because I don't have the urethral discipline I had when I was a youth.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about toilet seat not staying up
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed description of bathroom gymnastics and aging bladder control
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry overshare with clinical terminology making it funnier - 'urethral discipline' is comedy gold

jokes.text: Those pants go right in the incinerator. I swear to you, if those pants touch that floor, I'm going home in my underwear.
jokes.setup: Larry's germaphobia about bathroom floors
jokes.punchline: Extreme solution of burning pants and leaving in underwear
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurosis escalated to absurd extreme - incinerator and public underwear

jokes.text: 'I call her Deuce. Number two.' 'Mary Ferguson 2? That's right.'
jokes.setup: Jeff talking about his new girlfriend
jokes.punchline: The crude nickname and explanation
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but character-appropriate humor for Jeff

jokes.text: By the way, let me give you a little tip, okay? You're gonna be with this guy? Don't walk into any glass doors.
jokes.setup: Mary Ferguson going to Asia with new boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Random, oddly specific travel advice
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to give weirdly specific advice for unclear reasons

jokes.text: That dog is sittin' at home anticipating a doggie bag coming home. They're not expecting five slices. Got five slices there. What if you brought home three slices and gave me two?
jokes.setup: Mary refusing to share her steak because it's for her dog
jokes.punchline: Larry negotiating with detailed dog psychology and slice mathematics
jokes.explanation: Larry's bizarre logic about dog expectations and mathematical food distribution

jokes.text: But pets should take precedence over someone I just met.
jokes.setup: Larry arguing humans should take precedence over animals
jokes.punchline: Mary's cold logic about relationship hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly logical but harsh social hierarchy delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: 'I'm gonna give my dog five slices of my steak and not this guy.' 'You should pay for the bill.' That's wow. I got the wow.
jokes.setup: Argument over who has the bigger 'wow'
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic competition over who has the more outrageous position
jokes.explanation: Larry's competitive nature extending to who has the most outrageous grievance

jokes.text: So when you go there, keep an eye out. When you're at the dentist, you need to find out what's going on.
jokes.setup: Jeff suspicious about Angie's abortion complications
jokes.punchline: Assigning Larry as dental detective
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's investigative mission but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: What? What'd you say about my towels? Ratty towels? I have ratty towels? It's not a fact. My towels are not ratty.
jokes.setup: Susie casually mentioning Larry's old towels
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate defensive escalation about towel quality
jokes.explanation: Larry's defensiveness over something trivial - classic character response

jokes.text: Towels are better when they're washed over and over again, like a pair of jeans. They dry you a lot better. They're aged. Because new doesn't dry. You pat dry. I rub dry. That's the difference.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his old towels
jokes.punchline: Elaborate theory about towel aging and drying techniques
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry pseudo-science about towel quality with pat vs rub dry distinction

jokes.text: You wanna keep patting dry for the rest of your life, go pat dry because you don't know the pleasures of a rub dry.
jokes.setup: Larry's towel drying philosophy
jokes.punchline: Treating towel technique as life philosophy with pleasures unknown to pat-dryers
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry absurdity - towel drying as profound life choice with 'pleasures' - extremely quotable

jokes.text: Can we keep a couple aged? You see what you're doing now? You're turning him against my nice new towels.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking to keep some old towels
jokes.punchline: Susie seeing this as Jeff being corrupted by Larry
jokes.explanation: Larry's influence spreading like a virus - good callback structure

jokes.text: A little ratty. Like your towels. When I say ratty, I think of your towels.
jokes.setup: Cheryl describing the seedy venue
jokes.punchline: Connecting everything ratty to Larry's towels
jokes.explanation: Great callback - Larry's towels becoming universal reference for rattiness

jokes.text: 'A little ratty. Like your towels.' 'What? When I say ratty, I think of your towels.'
jokes.setup: Describing the seedy venue
jokes.punchline: Cheryl using Larry's towels as her go-to comparison for 'ratty'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the towel gag - everyone's talking about Larry's towels

jokes.text: No, I don't agree to disagree. I disagree to disagree.
jokes.setup: Cheryl trying to end towel argument diplomatically
jokes.punchline: Larry's refusing even the concept of agreeing to disagree
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - rejecting even the diplomatic solution. Extremely quotable wordplay.

jokes.text: My ears are bleeding. This is really one of the worst experiences of my life, I'm not exaggerating.
jokes.setup: Dylan's loud rock performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic reaction to normal concert volume
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Larry's earlier dread - his worst fears realized

jokes.text: I was clapping and swaying and clapping and swaying.
jokes.setup: Larry lying about enjoying the show
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed description of fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible acting and over-description of enthusiasm

jokes.text: You got fuckin' tissue in your ears. To block sound, possibly?
jokes.setup: Larry caught with tissue in his ears
jokes.punchline: Dylan immediately seeing through the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures getting caught in an obvious lie

jokes.text: There was a guy standing behind me. So loud. Screaming in my ear. And he was drowning out the show. And this way, I was able to drown him out and still get the essence.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to lie about the tissue
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate explanation involving fan drowning out band
jokes.explanation: Larry's lie becoming more complex and absurd - 'getting the essence' is funny

jokes.text: I've seen the one where you were in the kitchen cooking. The boat movie.
jokes.setup: Dylan asking what Larry has seen of his work
jokes.punchline: Larry making up nonexistent performances
jokes.explanation: Classic caught-in-a-lie escalation with random made-up details

jokes.text: You never cooked chicken alfredo with a friend?
jokes.setup: Dylan saying there's no kitchen scene
jokes.punchline: Larry doubling down with specific pasta dish
jokes.explanation: Specific chicken alfredo detail makes the lie funnier and more pathetic

jokes.text: In two words, it stunk. Go fuck yourself. I thought you said you could take it?
jokes.setup: Dylan asking for honest feedback about his performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty followed by Dylan's immediate reversal
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Dylan says he can take criticism then immediately proves he can't

jokes.text: I started a restaurant where you can sit and eat a meal with your dog next to you outside. First of its kind.
jokes.setup: Dylan claiming to be peaceful and kind
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have invented outdoor dining with dogs
jokes.explanation: Dylan's obvious lie about inventing something that's existed forever

jokes.text: I took an edible soon as the show ended and it's really starting to kick in, so I need to get in my zone, do some yoga. Do some MDMA.
jokes.setup: Dylan trying to end the conversation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of multiple drugs as routine self-care
jokes.explanation: Dylan's casual drug use as excuse to avoid Larry

jokes.text: See that palm? That's where he's gonna be eating out of very soon.
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing to apologize to Dylan
jokes.punchline: Executive's confident prediction about Larry's future subservience
jokes.explanation: Visual metaphor for power dynamics - eating out of someone's palm

jokes.text: No, no, she's doing Habitat for Humanity. In New Mexico. Well, it's close to Mexico. And she's got family across the border.
jokes.setup: Network asking where Maria Sofia is for retaping
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate lie about charitable work and family
jokes.explanation: Larry and Jeff building an elaborate cover story for nonexistent actress

jokes.text: Nobody's talked to me about a toilet that doesn't stay up. If it was broken, they would speak to me 'cause I fix everything in the building.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Julio about Don Jr. speaking to him
jokes.punchline: Julio revealing no one spoke to him, exposing Don Jr.'s lie
jokes.explanation: Reveals Don Jr.'s lie and sets up Larry's righteous indignation

jokes.text: If my arrival time was after my appointment time, I think I would lie about the arrival time. I'd feel like I'm not being a good patient.
jokes.setup: Receptionist asking for arrival time
jokes.punchline: Larry admitting he would lie about punctuality
jokes.explanation: Larry's honest dishonesty about hypothetical lying

jokes.text: Are they diamonds? Yes, they are. Means they're expensive. I know. I treated myself. I don't wanna feel guilty about it.
jokes.setup: Larry investigating Angie's expensive earrings
jokes.punchline: Angie's defensive response about treating herself
jokes.explanation: Larry's awkward investigation yielding suspicious evidence

jokes.text: How does it feel to know that nobody wants to see you?
jokes.setup: Dentist making small talk
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal philosophical question about dentistry
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry darkness - existential question about unwanted professions delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: You want me to turn down the Greek music? Yeah. This is a Greek office. It's the music of my ancestors.
jokes.setup: Larry asking to turn down music
jokes.punchline: Doctor's appeal to cultural heritage for dentist office music
jokes.explanation: Cultural pride vs patient comfort - reasonable conflict with humor

jokes.text: You're kickin' me out? Yes, I'm kicking you out. Fine, good. But you didn't finish! You gotta put the crown in. My mouth is no good.
jokes.setup: Doctor kicking Larry out over music complaint
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical concern about incomplete dental work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Curb escalation - principle vs practicality with Larry's incomplete mouth

jokes.text: What you should do is this, you send an email before you go to the dentist, let him know what you wanna hear. He plays what the fuck I tell him to play. It's like playlist courtesy.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about dentist music
jokes.punchline: Leon's solution of pre-dentist music negotiation
jokes.explanation: Leon's practical solution taken to absurd extreme - 'playlist courtesy'

jokes.text: One is for takin' off your clothes. And one is for puttin' clothes on.
jokes.setup: Leon describing stripper music vs Bossa Nova
jokes.punchline: Categorizing music by clothing removal vs addition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon logic - binary music classification system based on undressing

jokes.text: Angel Muffin? Angel Muffin? That's her name? His name. Him? Him? You named a male, Angel Muffin?
jokes.setup: Learning the dog's name
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror at masculine dog with feminine name
jokes.explanation: Larry's gender anxiety about dog names - classic neurosis

jokes.text: There's no gender to angels or muffins.
jokes.setup: Larry's objection to male dog named Angel Muffin
jokes.punchline: Logical but absurd defense of genderless angels and baked goods
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical response that's technically correct but highlights absurdity

jokes.text: That's like a five year old would name their dog that. I could never say that name out loud. It's too humiliating.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued objection to dog name
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to say the name due to embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Larry's masculine insecurity about saying cute dog name in public

jokes.text: Look at that. Now this is how your towels should feel.
jokes.setup: Dog vomiting on something soft
jokes.punchline: Connecting dog vomit texture to Larry's towel debate
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out callback connecting dog vomit to earlier towel argument

jokes.text: Who doesn't take a day off for an abortion? How do you get an abortion, then clean someone's teeth in the same day?
jokes.setup: Angie working without taking time off after abortion
jokes.punchline: Jeff's logical question about physical demands
jokes.explanation: Dark practical question about work ethic vs medical recovery

jokes.text: You wash, and you wash, you hit 'em against the rock, and then you age your towels until I get it right.
jokes.setup: Susie asking how Larry will make new towels ratty
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate towel aging process involving rocks
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry absurdity - primitive towel aging methods like winemaking

jokes.text: I dive into that dumpster. It was disgusting. I had ravioli all over me. I sift through the garbage, I come out with this amazing miracle.
jokes.setup: Dylan telling story of rescuing his dog from dumpster
jokes.punchline: Heroic rescue story with disgusting ravioli details
jokes.explanation: Dylan's dramatic rescue story with specific food waste details

jokes.text: Dumpster? 'Cause... I found him in the dumpster. You shouldn't talk to him like that.
jokes.setup: Dylan naming dog after where he found him
jokes.punchline: Larry thinking 'shut up' was directed at Dylan's explanation
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding about who Larry was addressing

jokes.text: Hey, buddy, what the fuck? Why didn't you call your dog's name? Couldn't say it.
jokes.setup: Larry's inability to say Angel Muffin throughout episode
jokes.punchline: His admission that he physically couldn't say the embarrassing name
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to running gag - Larry's masculinity preventing him from helping hurt dog

jokes.text: You know what? Forgot to mention, I spoke to the maintenance guy. And he said that you guys never spoke.
jokes.setup: Don Jr.'s lie about speaking to maintenance throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Larry confronting him with evidence of the lie
jokes.explanation: Satisfying confrontation with evidence - Larry's detective work paying off

jokes.text: Larry, are you calling me a liar? I don't know. Who am I supposed to believe, the maintenance guy or Don Jr.? What is that supposed to mean? You don't know what Don Jr. means?
jokes.setup: Don Jr. asking if Larry is calling him a liar
jokes.punchline: Larry's pointed reference to 'Don Jr.' meaning unreliable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry dig at nepotism and privilege with 'Don Jr.' reference

jokes.text: Mary Ferguson is gone, Larry. And it's your fault! That's two Mary Fergusons you cost me. Mary Fergusons don't fuckin' grow on trees.
jokes.setup: Leon losing his travel companion due to Larry's dog incident
jokes.punchline: Leon's dramatic lament about scarcity of Mary Fergusons
jokes.explanation: Leon's hilarious metaphor treating women named Mary Ferguson as rare commodity

jokes.text: 'Yeah, fuck that. I'm taking this shit to my fuckin' room. What are you doing? Give me those towels. What are you doing? Fuck you, Larry!'
jokes.setup: Larry appreciating the new soft towels
jokes.punchline: Leon stealing the towels and telling Larry off
jokes.explanation: Good Leon moment and callback to towel debate - he takes the good ones

jokes.text: Since that visit, I've been listening to Greek music and it turns out, you know what, I really like it.
jokes.setup: Larry returning to apologize to Greek dentist
jokes.punchline: Claiming he now loves the music he complained about
jokes.explanation: Larry's fake character growth to get his dental work finished

jokes.text: 'This Julio, he promised me, he looked me in the face and he said he was gonna fix the urinal and he didn't. He's a fuckin' liar! Every day, he promising me and he doesn't fix it.'
jokes.setup: Learning about the broken urinal
jokes.punchline: The dentist's angry rant about Julio, mirroring Larry's Don Jr. situation
jokes.explanation: Great parallel structure - the dentist has the same problem Larry had

jokes.text: You never lie to a Greek, especially the Greek mafia. I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Doctor angry about Julio the janitor lying
jokes.punchline: Casual joke about Greek mafia then immediate backtrack
jokes.explanation: Dentist's joke about his own ethnicity with quick clarification

jokes.text: She had an abortion last week. Good. But you know what? She didn't miss one day of work.
jokes.setup: Dentist explaining why Angie seems down
jokes.punchline: His 'Good' response followed by admiration for her work ethic
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark callback - confirms Jeff's suspicions while admiring Angie's dedication

tmdb_overview: Larry is forced to attend an ill-fated work meeting and investigates the truth behind a faulty toilet. Determined to unravel a mystery of his own, Jeff employs Larry's detective skills.
